title: Platform Outdoor Sectional
summary: A low, modular 2x6-decked sectional piece with a slatted seat surface and a solid back, built to combine with matching units into a full sectional.
description:
This piece is built as one module of a larger sectional — the base is a simple 2x4 frame, decked in 2x6 boards with a consistent 1/2" gap for drainage and a clean, deliberate look. Working from the back inward as you attach the decking keeps your gaps consistent across the whole surface instead of compounding a small error board by board.

The back is genuinely open on one end by design, since it's meant to butt up against a neighboring sectional piece. If you're using this as a standalone piece rather than as part of a full sectional, a metal L bracket on the inside of that open end adds real security without changing the look from the outside.

materials.name: Metal L brackets
materials.note: Optional — adds extra security to the open end if not butted against another sectional piece.

cutList.note: You most likely won't need to cut these full-length boards.
You most likely won't need to cut these full-length boards.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two base side rails at 96" and four base cross members at 31-1/4" from the 2x4. Cut nine decking boards at 96" and three back boards at 37" from the 2x6. Cut six top cleats at 16" from the 2x2. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to four 10 ft 2x4 and ten 10 ft 2x6. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.

steps.title: Build the base
steps.body:
Build the base frame from the side rails and cross members. Pocket holes (1-1/2" setting, 2-1/2" pocket-hole screws) work well, or you can countersink 2-1/2" self-tapping exterior screws instead. Use glue throughout and check for square as you go.

If you're using pocket holes, consider also drilling a set facing upward now for attaching the decking boards in the next step — it saves you working underneath later.

steps.title: Attach the decking
steps.body: Attach the decking boards starting at the back and working inward, leaving a 1/2" gap between each board.

steps.title: Attach the top cleats
steps.body: Attach the 2x2 cleats to the top of the decking using the 1-1/2" pocket-hole setting, 2-1/2" pocket-hole screws, and glue.

steps.title: Attach the back boards
steps.body:
Attach the back board to both the 2x2 cleat and the 2x6 decking below it. Then split the remaining distance evenly and attach the center back boards.

We felt this back was plenty sturdy as built, but if you're concerned about the open end of the sectional, add a metal L bracket on the inside for extra security.

images.alt: Low platform outdoor sectional module with slatted seat and solid back
